steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says ephemeral objects "are useful as evidence of cultural change" and the blank completes: "they can ______ shifts in norms, values, and concerns." The blank needs a phrase meaning "serve as evidence of" or "bear witness to" those shifts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘dissociate from’: Means to separate or detach from something. This says the objects would distance themselves from cultural shifts — the opposite of serving as evidence of them. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘collude with’: Means to secretly cooperate, implying wrongdoing or conspiracy. Ephemeral objects do not conspire with cultural shifts. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘compensate for’: Means to make up for a deficiency. The passage frames these objects as revealing information, not offsetting any lack. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘attest to’: Means to provide evidence of or bear witness to — exactly what the passage describes: ephemeral objects serving as evidence of cultural change that traditional sources may miss. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
